---
title: "Solution 1A — Example data card (gene simulation)"
format: html
---

# Data card — synthetic gene × trait / disease (example answer)

Full instructor cards: [gene × trait](../../data/cards/gene-trait-simulation.qmd) · [gene × disease](../../data/cards/gene-disease-simulation.qmd) · [Data catalog](../../data/index.qmd)

## 1. Source

Rows are **simulated** in R (`set.seed(420)` for trait; `set.seed(123)` for disease). No human or animal measurements. Ground truth: **GeneA** and **GeneB** affect continuous **trait**; **Gene1**, **Gene3**, **Gene4** (and Gene1×Gene3 interaction) affect binary **disease_presence**.

## 2. Outcome

- **Trait:** continuous (arbitrary units).
- **Disease:** factor `Absent` / `Present`.

## 3. Predictors

Ten gene expression columns (`GeneA`–`GeneJ` or `Gene1`–`Gene10`); numeric, standardized in some models.

## 4. Sample size

Trait: *n* ≈ 150; disease: *n* = 200. No missing values in simulation.

## 5. Leakage risks

- Fitting and evaluating on the **same rows** (in-sample R² / accuracy) overstates performance.
- Choosing lasso λ or AIC on the **same data** used for final coefficients.
- **Day 2 fix:** hold-out test set; cross-validation inside training; for microbiome, **group by mouse Individual**.

## 6. Subgroups

Not applicable in simulation; in real data we would record batch, sex, and site.

---

*Students should write in their own words; this is one acceptable instructor example.*
